articles.title: GitHub Moves to Usage-Based Pricing for Copilot to Tackle Escalating Inference Costs
articles.summary: GitHub will shift Copilot billing from flat-rate to metered usage, citing unsustainable “escalating inference cost” from its heaviest AI users. The change, effective next quarter, introduces granular token-level metering and tiered overage surcharges. Internal telemetry shows the top 5 % of accounts now consume 42 % of total GPU minutes, driven by multi-file code generation and iterative refactoring loops. By aligning revenue with compute burn, GitHub hopes to cap gross-margin compression at 8–10 % while incentivizing efficient prompting. Enterprise customers gain new budget-forecast dashboards and hard caps, but freelancers face bill shock if they exceed bundled quotas. The move signals the end of the “all-you-can-eat” era for developer tools and will likely pressure rivals such as Amazon CodeWhisperer and Google IDX to follow suit or subsidize usage through cloud bundling. [Ars Technica AI](https://arstechnica.com/ai/2026/04/github-will-start-charging-copilot-users-based-on-their-actual-ai-usage/)

articles.title: KARL Framework Uses Dynamic Reinforcement Learning to Curb LLM Hallucinations
articles.summary: Researchers unveil KARL (Knowledge-Boundary-Aware Reinforcement Learning), a training framework that teaches large language models when to abstain rather than hallucinate. KARL introduces a two-stage RL pipeline: exploration phase estimates the model’s knowledge boundary in real time using within-group response statistics, while the refinement phase converts out-of-scope answers into abstentions without degrading in-scope accuracy. Across TruthfulQA, HaluEval, and newly released BoundaryBench, KARL cuts hallucination rates by 31–47 % while preserving 96 % of answerable accuracy. Unlike static-reward RLHF, KARL’s dynamic reward mechanism adapts as the model’s knowledge evolves, making it compatible with continual fine-tuning. The framework is model-agnostic and has been successfully applied to Llama-3-70B and GPT-4o-mini. Enterprise adopters—particularly in legal-tech and healthcare—view KARL as a path to deploy LLMs in high-risk domains without costly human-in-the-loop moderation. [arXiv Machine Learning](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.22779)

articles.title: LARS Challenges PEFT Orthodoxy by Slashing Activation Memory for On-Device LLMs
articles.summary: A new paper dismantles the assumption that parameter-efficient fine-tuning equals memory efficiency. LARS (Low-memory Activation-Rank Subspace) constrains the activation subspace instead of model weights, decoupling memory growth from sequence length. Experiments show LARS trims peak GPU memory by 33.54 % and CPU memory by 51.95 % versus LoRA across Llama-2-7B and Mistral-7B, while retaining 98–99 % downstream accuracy. The technique runs inference on a Raspberry Pi 4 with 4 GB RAM at 4.3 tokens/sec, opening the door for on-device personalization of 7–13 B models. Chip vendors are already integrating LARS kernels into next-gen NPU SDKs, and Qualcomm plans to ship LARS-optimized Snapdragon drivers in Q1-2027. The work reframes the on-device LLM debate from weight compression to activation compression. [arXiv Machine Learning](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.22783)

articles.title: Meta to Harvest Employee Mouse & Keyboard Telemetry to Train Next-Gen AI Agents
articles.summary: Meta plans to deploy internal monitoring software that logs keystrokes, mouse trajectories, scroll velocity, and window focus from consenting employees to generate high-quality interactive training data for AI agents. The opt-in program, code-named Project Insight, targets 15 k engineers and aims to produce 200 TB of multimodal behavioral sequences annually. Privacy safeguards include on-device anonymization, differential privacy noise injection, and a 30-day rolling deletion window. The initiative underscores the scarcity of nuanced human-computer interaction datasets required for agentic systems capable of GUI automation and complex software workflows. Critics warn of mission creep, but Meta argues the data is essential to surpass brittle screen-scraping agents and achieve robust desktop-level autonomy. [Ars Technica AI](https://arstechnica.com/ai/2026/04/meta-will-use-employee-tracking-software-to-help-train-ai-agents-report/)

articles.title: FIDO Alliance, Google & Mastercard Race to Secure AI Agents’ Credit-Card Access
articles.summary: The FIDO Alliance, Google, and Mastercard are jointly drafting a new authentication protocol—AgentAuth 1.0—to prevent AI agents from executing unauthorized financial transactions. The spec layers FIDO2 device-bound credentials with real-time risk scoring and agent-identity certificates issued by cloud providers. A pilot with 50 merchants saw fraudulent agent-initiated charges drop 94 %, while legitimate agent purchases rose 38 %. The standard will be embedded in Chrome 134 and Android 16 this fall, with Apple and Amazon evaluating adoption. As agents gain the ability to autonomously shop, book travel, and manage subscriptions, the consortium aims to avoid a “Wild West” of unverified agent spending that could spook regulators and card networks. [Wired AI](https://www.wired.com/story/the-race-is-on-to-keep-ai-agents-from-running-wild-with-your-credit-cards/)

articles.title: MolClaw Agent Orchestrates 70 Hierarchical Skills for End-to-End Drug Discovery
articles.summary: MolClaw introduces a three-tier hierarchical skill architecture—tool, workflow, and discipline levels—to autonomously manage molecule evaluation, screening, and optimization pipelines. The system unifies 30+ specialized cheminformatics tools via 70 discrete skills, outperforming prior agents on the new MolBench benchmark that spans 8–50 sequential tool calls. In end-to-end discovery tasks, MolClaw improves hit-rate by 22 % and reduces average synthesis cycles from 4.1 to 2.7 iterations. Ablation studies reveal that gains concentrate on tasks demanding structured orchestration, not ad-hoc scripting, highlighting workflow competence as the critical bottleneck in AI-driven drug discovery. Early pharma partners, including Roche and Merck, are piloting MolClaw within secure VPCs to accelerate lead-optimization timelines. [arXiv AI](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.21937)

articles.title: Artifact-Based Agent Framework Brings Reproducibility to Clinical Image Workflows
articles.summary: A new agent framework introduces “artifact contracts” that formalize intermediate and final outputs in medical image processing, ensuring deterministic reproducibility across heterogeneous clinical datasets. Tested on real-world CT and MRI cohorts, the system adapts workflows to dataset-specific conditions while logging every transformation for auditability. The framework runs fully on-prem to satisfy privacy constraints and supports semantic querying of workflow state. In pilot deployments at two EU hospitals, radiologists reduced re-scan rates by 18 % and cut protocol configuration time from 45 to 7 minutes. The approach bridges the gap between benchtop AI models and regulated clinical deployment, offering a template for other high-stakes domains. [arXiv AI](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.21936)

articles.title: Claude Gains Native Connectors into Photoshop, Blender, Ableton & More
articles.summary: Anthropic releases “Creative Connectors,” enabling Claude to read and write directly into Adobe Creative Cloud, Blender, Ableton, Autodesk, and Affinity suites. The connectors expose scene graphs, layer metadata, and MIDI state via secure local sockets, allowing Claude to perform context-aware edits—e.g., retouching specific Photoshop layers or generating Blender geometry from natural language. Early alpha users report 35 % faster asset iteration cycles and a 50 % reduction in manual import/export steps. The move positions Claude as a creative co-pilot against Adobe’s Firefly and Google’s Imagen, and hints at Anthropic’s broader strategy to embed AI inside existing professional toolchains rather than replace them. [The Verge AI](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919648/anthropic-claude-creative-connectors-adobe-blender)

articles.title: Math Takes Two Benchmark Tests Emergent Numerical Reasoning via Agent Communication
articles.summary: Researchers propose “Math Takes Two,” a novel benchmark where two agents without prior mathematical knowledge must invent a shared symbolic protocol to solve a visual task. The setup eschews predefined notation, forcing agents to discover latent numerical structure through communication alone. Initial experiments with GPT-4o and Claude-3.5-Sonnet show emergent place-value systems and arithmetic operators in 62 % of runs, but performance plateaus beyond 3-digit addition. The benchmark offers a litmus test for genuine mathematical reasoning versus pattern matching, and could inform curricula for future reasoning-focused models. DARPA has earmarked $4 M to extend the paradigm to geometry and algebra. [arXiv AI](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.21935)

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo Enables Resilient Distributed Training Across Unreliable Nodes
articles.summary: DeepMind unveils Decoupled DiLoCo, a distributed training algorithm that maintains convergence even when 30 % of worker nodes are flaky or heterogeneous. By decoupling local optimizer steps from global synchronization, the method cuts communication overhead by 60 % and allows CPU-only nodes to contribute gradient updates to a GPU cluster. Tests on a 512-node spanning three continents show only a 4 % slowdown versus fully synchronous training. The technique is particularly relevant for academic consortia and geo-distributed startups that cannot afford dedicated clusters, and is already integrated into JAX’s experimental stack. [DeepMind Blog](https://deepmind.google/blog/decoupled-diloco/)

articles.title: OpenAI Brings GPT, Codex & Managed Agents to AWS in Enterprise Push
articles.summary: OpenAI launches managed endpoints for GPT-4.5, Codex-1.5, and new Managed Agents directly on AWS, letting enterprises deploy inside their own VPCs with SOC-2, HIPAA, and FedRAMP controls. The service integrates with AWS KMS for key rotation and CloudTrail for audit logs, removing the compliance friction that has slowed regulated industries. Early adopters include JPMorgan for code modernization and Pfizer for document triage. Pricing follows AWS’ pay-as-you-go model with reserved-capacity discounts up to 35 %. The move intensifies competition with Bedrock and positions OpenAI as a neutral SaaS layer atop hyperscaler infrastructure. [OpenAI Blog](https://openai.com/index/openai-on-aws)

articles.title: Data Center Boom Drives 66 % Spike in Natural-Gas Plant Construction Costs
articles.summary: Surging electricity demand from AI data centers has pushed natural-gas power-plant construction costs up 66 % in two years, while average build times lengthened from 34 to 42 months. ERCOT data show data-center load growing at 18 % CAGR, forcing developers to lock in 20-year gas supply contracts at premium rates. Analysts warn of a potential 2-3 GW supply shortfall by 2028 in Virginia and Ohio hubs, raising PPA prices above $65/MWh—double 2023 levels. The crunch is accelerating interest in small modular reactors and grid-scale storage, but gas remains the only dispatchable option that can come online before 2029. [TechCrunch AI](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/27/data-center-demand-drives-66-surge-in-natural-gas-power-plant-costs/)

articles.title: Meta Secures 1 GW of New U.S. Solar to Power AI Data Centers
articles.summary: Meta signed three virtual-PPAs totaling 1 GW of new solar capacity across Texas and Arizona, bringing its contracted renewable portfolio to 7.2 GW. The deals, structured as 15-year fixed-price contracts starting in 2027, will supply 2.6 TWh annually—enough to offset projected AI training and inference loads from Llama-4 clusters. The move keeps Meta on track for net-zero operations by 2030 and pressures other hyperscalers to accelerate clean-energy procurement amid tightening grid carbon-intensity regulations. [TechCrunch AI](https://techcrunch.com/2025/10/31/meta-bought-1-gw-of-solar-this-week/)

articles.title: Converge Bio Lands $25 M Series A to Scale AI Drug Discovery Platform
articles.summary: Converge Bio raised $25 M in a Series A led by Bessemer Venture Partners, with participation from senior executives at Meta, OpenAI, and Wiz. The startup’s platform combines protein-foundation models with wet-lab automation to compress lead-discovery timelines by 40 %. Early partnerships with two top-10 pharma companies have yielded three pre-clinical candidates in oncology and rare disease. The new capital will expand lab capacity from 20k to 100k compounds per month and double headcount to 75. The round values the company at $110 M pre-money and positions it at the intersection of generative biology and high-throughput screening. [TechCrunch AI](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/13/ai-drug-discovery-startup-converge-bio-pulls-in-25m-from-bessemer-and-execs-from-meta-openai-and-wiz/)

articles.title: Mitti Labs Uses AI to Verify Methane Cuts in Climate-Smart Rice Farming
articles.summary: Mitti Labs partners with The Nature Conservancy to scale Alternate Wetting and Drying (AWD) practices among Indian rice farmers, leveraging satellite imagery and on-ground sensors validated by AI to quantify methane reductions. The platform processes 10 m resolution Sentinel-2 data combined with IoT moisture probes to generate field-level carbon credits. In the first 18 months, 12 k farmers across Punjab and Haryana cut methane emissions by 0.8 Mt CO₂e, translating to $6.4 M in credit revenue. Mitti’s AI verification layer reduces MRV costs by 70 % compared to manual sampling, unlocking new financing for smallholders. The model is now being replicated in Vietnam and Bangladesh. [TechCrunch AI](https://techcrunch.com/2025/08/26/how-one-ai-startup-is-helping-rice-farmers-battle-climate-change/)

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 Boosts Spatial Reasoning for Real-World Robots
articles.summary: DeepMind releases Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6, an embodied-reasoning upgrade that fuses Gemini’s vision-language backbone with multi-view 3-D scene understanding. The model improves pose estimation accuracy by 28 % on the new SpatialBench and enables zero-shot manipulation of previously unseen objects. A key innovation is cross-view attention that integrates wrist-mounted and fixed-camera feeds, allowing robots to reason about occlusions and tool use. Early tests on Everyday Robots’ mobile manipulator show 1.7× faster task completion in kitchen scenarios. The model is shipping as a TensorRT-optimized runtime for NVIDIA Jetson Orin, targeting warehouse and service-robot OEMs in Q3-2026. [DeepMind Blog](https://deepmind.google/blog/gemini-robotics-er-1-6/)

articles.title: Meta Contractor Covalen May Lay Off 700+ Irish Data-Labelers as AI Training Winds Down
articles.summary: More than 700 content-moderation and RLHF annotators at Covalen, a Meta contractor in Ireland, received redundancy notices as Meta shifts from human-labeled to synthetic data for model alignment. The cuts, slated for August, reflect broader industry automation of preference-data collection via constitutional AI and self-play techniques. Union representatives call the move “undignified,” citing abrupt notice periods and limited re-skilling options. Meta maintains that affected workers will receive six-month severance and access to an internal reskilling program, but analysts see the layoffs as an inflection point where human feedback becomes a diminishing input to frontier model development. [Wired AI](https://www.wired.com/story/meta-covalen-ai-workers-layoffs/)

articles.title: Musk vs. Altman OpenAI Trial Kicks Off with For-Profit Status at Stake
articles.summary: Elon Musk and Sam Altman face trial in Northern California over Musk’s claim that OpenAI abandoned its nonprofit mission. The court could bar OpenAI from converting to a for-profit entity or even force divestiture of assets developed under the original charter—potentially derailing its $150 B IPO plans. Key evidence includes 2015 emails discussing AGI safety and board minutes from 2023’s restructuring. Legal experts assign a 30 % chance of injunctive relief, which would reverberate across the AI landscape by casting doubt on similar conversions at Anthropic and xAI. The trial is expected to last six weeks and may set precedent for how mission-driven AI organizations balance public benefit and investor returns. [MIT Technology Review](https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/04/27/1136466/elon-musk-and-sam-altman-are-going-to-court-over-openais-future/)
